item.name: Aliens Attack!
item.comment:
Planetary Defence cards felt uninteresting to me, and the final battle seemed heavily luck-driven. I also found some of the rules during the final battle unclear. For example, what does "teleport a ship to the end of the deck" mean when you're fighting the Mothership? Or when "The Mothership directs another enemy ship to move down 4 spaces," which ship is supposed to move?
The idea behind the game is good, but the implementation didn't work for me. I also found the artwork tiring on the eyes, although that may just be a matter of personal taste.
I was hoping for a nice light puzzle game with interesting decisions to make on a weekday evening, but it ended up feeling more like, "Okay, fire this, flip this card, move two cards down..."
Sorry if this sounds harsh. You may end up loving it, I just didn't enjoy it.

item.name: The Crew: The Quest for Planet Nine
Dice Pool Party
item.comment: How they were NOT able to match the colors of the dice to the colors on the score sheet is beyond me. Dice is yellow, score sheet has orange. Pinks do not match either. Game is fun but this color mismatch is annoying.

item.name: EXIT: The Game – The Mysterious Museum
item.comment: Some puzzles are just way too easy and others are very very difficult. There is no story. It is just "ok what is the next puzzle?". And they are not even fun. Also why can't they give a few copies of the cards you need to destroy so you can play a few times? Only a handful of components need to be cut but everything else goes into trash. Just give a few copies of those few components we need to cut/tear etc so make it at least playable a few times in the family!

item.name: Fantasy Realms: Greek Legends
item.comment: Original Fantasy Realms and Marvem Remix are simpler and better.

item.name: FateFlip: Washed Ashore
item.comment: Most of the time the story makes zero sense due to card shuffling. Idea is good but in practice you are just reading a bunch of related but inconsistent storyline.

item.name: For Northwood! A Solo Trick-Taking Game
Forest Shuffle
item.comment:
Since you can usually grab whatever you want from the clearing, the "what card to pay with" decision rarely feels tense. You often just pick up the same card you played a turn earlier, and your opponent usually doesn't care, they're doing the same thing.
The game also feels way too long for what it offers. Once you choose a direction (say, going for birds), you're basically doing the same thing for the next 30 minutes, planting trees and adding birds to them, with little variation.
I like the idea behind the game, building a forest ecosystem sounds great, and the first 10–15 minutes are indeed fun. But it quickly becomes repetitive and overstays its welcome.
On top of that, cards that let you draw with effects like "whenever you play a tree card" feel unbalanced. If someone gets those early, they gain a huge advantage since they no longer need to spend turns drawing.
Overall: a mediocre experience for me. Nice theme and artwork, but too long, too repetitive, and lacking real tension in the choices.

item.name: Harmonies
item.comment: It is a nice somewhat a thinky game. Your score will heavily depend on the available cards. Solving the puzzle is fun. When things work out it is very satisfying. Solo is also fun. I would even say solo is the best for this game.

item.name: Infinity Gauntlet: A Love Letter Game
King of Tokyo: Duel – Tokyo Bay
Machi Koro: The Harbor & Millionaire's Row Expansions
item.comment: Not sure why it is so highly rated. Ruins the game completely. Avoid.

item.name: Point City
item.comment: Honestly it is just selecting what is available. The last 2 rounds decide who wins based on what is available in the market and whether you can get them or not. Still a nice way to spend time as a family. More of a relaxing and casual let's see what happens game.

item.name: Star Wars: Jabba's Palace – A Love Letter Game
Swish
item.comment: It was much more fun than I expected. Allowing swishes with 2 cards make the game too easy. Playing with "at least 3 swishes" makes it quite fun.

item.name: The Thing That Should Not Be
World Wonders
item.comment:
House Rule: Extended Wonder Preview
At all times, six World Wonder cards are kept face-up and visible.
Players may only purchase World Wonders from the top three cards, following the normal rules and costs.
When a World Wonder is purchased, immediately slide the remaining cards up and reveal a new card so that six Wonders are always visible.
Purpose: This rule reduces forced or unmeaningful actions and allows players to plan ahead more strategically, without changing competition or purchasing limits.

item.name: Machi Koro: The Harbor & Millionaire's Row Expansions
item.comment: Not sure why it is so highly rated. Ruins the game completely. Avoid.
